families.name: Optimization

families.name: Coding
families.description: Syndrome, entropy, polar, LDPC, RS, BCH, fountain

families.name: Statistical inference

families.name: Combinatorial
families.description: Generating functions, Möbius, matroid, RSK, Burnside

families.name: Computational geometry

families.name: Number theory

families.name: Differential geometry

families.name: Formal languages

families.name: Mathematical physics
families.description: Partition fn, OPE, lattice gauge, Wilson loop, RG

primitives.name: Bitwise logic
primitives.silicon_target: Standard logic gates (universal); XOR/NOT/SWAP are L₀

primitives.name: Lattice reduction
primitives.silicon_target: Gram-Schmidt + size-reduce + swap engine — post-quantum crypto core

primitives.name: Discrete log oracle
primitives.silicon_target: DLOG oracle (Shor on quantum, sieves on classical)

primitives.name: Quasigroup op
primitives.silicon_target: Lookup table or algebraic unit (Latin square)

primitives.name: CRT (Chinese Remainder)
primitives.silicon_target: Modular arithmetic unit with CRT reconstruction

primitives.name: Krylov subspace gen
primitives.silicon_target: SpMV engine with state buffer for basis vectors

primitives.name: Differentiation
primitives.silicon_target: Finite difference engine / AD tape unit

primitives.name: Greeks computation
primitives.silicon_target: AD tape applied to pricing model

primitives.name: Incomplete factorization
primitives.silicon_target: Sparse triangular engine with fill control

primitives.name: Dirac operator application
primitives.silicon_target: Spinor-field stencil engine; γ-matrix multiply pipeline on bundle sections

primitives.name: Cyclic cohomology
primitives.silicon_target: Cyclic-complex reduction engine (Connes b, B operators)

primitives.name: Lattice gauge sweep (HMC)
primitives.silicon_target: SU(3) link-update engine + MD integrator (every lattice site in parallel)

primitives.name: Wilson loop evaluation
primitives.silicon_target: Ordered SU(3) product accumulator + trace unit

primitives.name: Fermion matrix inversion
primitives.silicon_target: Sparse complex CG/multigrid for lattice Dirac operator — **the lattice QCD bottleneck**

primitives.name: Monodromy computation
primitives.silicon_target: Holonomy accumulator on fiber bundle (path-ordered transport)

primitives.name: BCH decoding
primitives.silicon_target: Syndrome computation + error locator polynomial unit

primitives.name: Reed-Muller decoding
primitives.silicon_target: Fast Hadamard transform + majority logic decoder

primitives.name: BCJR (turbo) decoding
primitives.silicon_target: Forward-backward trellis with extrinsic information exchange

primitives.name: Fountain (LT/Raptor) decoding
primitives.silicon_target: Belief propagation on rateless code graph

primitives.name: Discrete Gaussian sampling (lattice crypto)
primitives.silicon_target: Rejection sampler over discrete Gaussian — Kyber/Dilithium signature core (distinct from #17 LLL which reduces the basis)

primitives.name: Isogeny step (Vélu)
primitives.silicon_target: Vélu formula evaluator over isogeny graph (CSIDH/SIDH)

primitives.name: ZK-friendly hash (Poseidon/MiMC)
primitives.silicon_target: Sponge construction over prime-field S-boxes — every zkSNARK gadget (distinct from #71 pairing)

primitives.name: Verifiable delay function (VDF)
primitives.silicon_target: Iterated squaring in class group (intentionally serial)

primitives.name: Robust M-estimator
primitives.silicon_target: Iteratively reweighted least squares with influence function (Huber/Tukey)

primitives.name: Column generation
primitives.silicon_target: Pricing subproblem solver + master LP updater

primitives.name: Simulated annealing
primitives.silicon_target: Random move + Metropolis criterion + cooling schedule

primitives.name: AKS primality (deterministic)
primitives.silicon_target: Polynomial congruence checker over Z[x]/(x^r-1, n)

primitives.name: Index calculus
primitives.silicon_target: Smooth relation collector + linear algebra over Z/p

primitives.name: Lucas-Lehmer (Mersenne)
primitives.silicon_target: Iterated squaring mod 2^p − 1 with FFT-based multiply

primitives.name: Permanent (Ryser)
primitives.silicon_target: Subset-sum permanent engine (#P-hard; boson sampling core)

primitives.name: RSK correspondence
primitives.silicon_target: Insertion/bumping engine — bijection between permutations and Young tableau pairs

composition.label: XOR+AND → adder
mul+add → FMA
FMA grid → matmul
accumulation in matmul
QK^T and V projection
softmax in attention
butterfly = FMA + twiddle
index permutation in FFT
integer arith mod q → NTT
conv theorem: FFT+mul+iFFT
NTT in FHE bootstrap
poly ring in FHE bootstrap
AD tape in MCMC
RNG in MCMC
accept/reject in MCMC
logsumexp in message passing
marginalize in message passing
tropical DP in Viterbi
scan in Viterbi
field arith in pairing (Miller loop)
EC ops in pairing
GF arith in syndrome compute
BP in LDPC decode
Shor via quantum gates solves DLP
filtered backprojection uses FFT
matmul as tensor contraction case
lattice meet/join is implemented as comparison + selection
quasigroup ops decompose into integer arith on Latin-square encoding
CRT reconstruction uses modular exponentiation for inverses
Givens / Householder rotations in QR/LU/SVD are FMA-based
matrix decomposition operates on a dense matrix product structure
QR algorithm for eigendecomposition iterates QR factorization
the iterative kernel of every eigensolver is dense matmul
Krylov subspace generation iterates sparse matrix-vector product
Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization at each step uses reduction (inner products)
DCT butterfly is a real-valued FMA tree
Hilbert transform = FFT → phase shift → iFFT
Laplace/Z-transform reduces to FFT after suitable substitution
Kleene star is the (min,+) tropical matrix exponentiation
transitive closure iterates Boolean / tropical matmul
special functions (Bessel, erf, gamma) are evaluated via CORDIC or polynomial FMA
polynomial summation uses reduction
persistent homology reduces a boundary matrix using Smith normal form
graph isomorphism reduces to canonical-form join over labelled subgraphs
color-refinement isomorphism is constraint propagation on vertex partitions
exterior product is the antisymmetrized geometric algebra product
simplicial complex construction joins point neighborhoods into faces
filtration is a parameterized cascade of simplicial complexes
functor application unifies object/morphism mappings
natural transformation is a family of morphisms commuting with two functors
adjunction resolution is the natural bijection between functor hom-sets
comultiplication is the categorical dual of FHRR bind (which IS multiplication)
Hopf antipode is the inverse element under FHRR-style binding
finite-difference differentiation = integer subtract + divide
AD-tape differentiation accumulates FMA in reverse
quadrature is a weighted FMA over sample points
the accumulator phase of integration is reduction
Itô integral is integration over a Brownian path
Itô integration draws Brownian increments from RNG
variational derivative is functional differentiation
MLE/MAP integrates the log-likelihood over data
MAP optimization uses activation-style soft constraints
Bayesian posterior update is a matmul of prior × likelihood
the posterior is normalized via softmax-style log-sum-exp
PCA / ICA is eigendecomposition of the data covariance matrix
kernel density estimation integrates a kernel over samples
natural gradient inverts the Fisher information matrix
generating-function coefficient extraction is symbolic term rewriting
Möbius inversion is a poset-shaped Möbius/zeta transform
matroid independence oracle uses constraint propagation on rank
beta reduction is term rewriting with the substitution rule
type inference unifies type variables across an expression tree
Hindley-Milner inference propagates type constraints
Brownian increment draws a Gaussian sample from RNG
Kalman predict-update is a sequence of matrix multiplications
the covariance update step uses Cholesky / matrix decomposition
particle filter resamples particles via Monte Carlo accept/reject
particle weights are perturbed by RNG draws
knot invariants (Jones, HOMFLY) compute via skein-relation rewriting
homology computation reduces a boundary matrix via Smith normal form
Clebsch-Gordan decomposition is a structured tensor product
C-G coefficients live in the non-abelian group Fourier framework
character of a representation is the trace (a reduction)
full Clifford product extends geometric algebra to all multivector grades
branch and bound is SAT-style search with cost-pruning
evolutionary selection draws from RNG for crossover and mutation
convex hull algorithms (Graham scan, Andrew monotone chain) start with sort
Voronoi/Delaunay can be lifted from convex hull in one higher dimension
point location queries traverse a Voronoi or trapezoidal subdivision
Boolean CSG resolves edge intersections via comparison + segmentation
Nash equilibrium computation reduces to LP / Lemke-Howson pivots
minimax / alpha-beta is comparison-driven game-tree pruning
VCG-style mechanism design solves a Nash subproblem at each node
Miller-Rabin primality test uses modular exponentiation
modular exponentiation underlies sieve and Pollard factoring
Euclidean GCD is a chain of integer divisions
continued fractions are produced by iterating GCD
parallel transport is FMA over Christoffel symbols along a path
the connection coefficients are partial derivatives of the metric
geodesic equation is a second-order ODE on the manifold
geodesic = parallel transport of velocity along itself
curvature tensor is built from second derivatives of the metric
exponential map shoots a geodesic from a tangent vector
parsing runs a finite-automaton step at each input symbol
NFA→DFA subset construction is constraint propagation over reachable states
language reversal swaps the automaton's source and accept states
sparse recovery (BPDN) uses the proximal operator on the L1 norm
RIP check verifies a norm-preservation property of a measurement matrix
Buchberger / F4 reduction is symbolic term rewriting on polynomials
variety intersection eliminates variables via Gröbner reduction
sheaf cohomology computes homology of the Čech complex
the partition function Z = Σ exp(-βH) is a reduction over states
the operator product expansion is symbolic series manipulation
TQFT cobordism composition is functor application from Bord to Vect
Feynman diagram generation is symbolic rewriting of interaction vertices
each diagram contributes a loop integral
Runge-Kutta / Verlet integration is a structured FMA stencil
ODE integration computes derivatives at each step
Lyapunov exponent integrates a tangent ODE alongside the main flow
Poincaré section samples crossings of a flow
bifurcation detection sweeps a parameter while integrating the flow
linear stability analysis uses eigendecomposition of the Jacobian
attractor characterization analyzes the long-time orbit of an integrator
Lebesgue integration generalizes the Riemann sum reduction
Radon-Nikodym derivative is the density of one Lebesgue measure w.r.t. another
conditional expectation is integration restricted to a sub-σ-algebra
spectral decomposition of an operator is its eigendecomposition
functional calculus applies a function to operator eigenvalues
QEC syndrome extraction multiplies stabilizer generators in GF(2)
Pauli correction is a GF(2)-linear operation
logical qubit encoding precomputes the syndrome map
Black-Scholes is a parabolic PDE in price and time
the BS solver integrates over time steps
the Greeks are partial derivatives of the option price
reaction-diffusion is a PDE with diffusion (Laplacian) and reaction terms
sequence alignment dynamic programming runs in the tropical (max,+) semiring
phylogenetic tree search optimizes alignment across taxa
Bayesian phylogenetics samples trees via Monte Carlo
FEM stiffness assembly is a sparse matmul over element basis functions
element integrals use quadrature
multigrid restriction is a weighted reduction onto the coarse grid
multigrid prolongation is a weighted FMA interpolation onto the fine grid
the right-hand side of a PDE step computes spatial derivatives
implicit PDE steps solve a linear system (matmul-based)
spectral methods diagonalize a PDE in Fourier space
spherical harmonics is the Fourier transform on SO(3)
graph Fourier basis = eigenvectors of the graph Laplacian
sparse format conversion is structured scatter/gather
incomplete factorization truncates a sparse LU
fill-in reordering sorts vertices by a heuristic minimum-degree score
max-flow / min-cut runs in the tropical (min,+) semiring
graph coloring is constraint propagation over color domains
spectral graph partitioning uses the Fiedler vector from the graph Fourier basis
ADMM iterates the proximal operator on each subproblem
interior point methods solve KKT systems via matrix factorization
polar successive-cancellation decoding inherits from generic syndrome decoding
LDPC belief propagation is sum-product on a Tanner graph
ZK proofs operate over polynomial commitments in finite fields
Groth16-style ZK proofs use elliptic-curve operations
Fiat-Shamir randomness comes from a hash
ZK verification uses elliptic-curve scalar multiplications
Groth16 verification uses bilinear pairing
MPC rounds compute over secret-shared finite-field elements
MPC round commitments use a hash
LMS adaptive filter step is a gradient update on filter weights
the filter output is a vector inner product (matmul row)
beamforming is a weighted matmul of array signals
frequency-domain beamforming uses FFT
OFDM modulation is an IFFT; demodulation is an FFT
Riccati equation solving uses Schur or matrix factorization
the Riccati operator is a matmul-heavy update
MPC step solves a horizon QP via proximal updates
the QP solver factorizes a Hessian-like matrix
PID = proportional + integral + derivative, all FMA
KL divergence sums log-ratios (a reduction)
the log-ratio comes from a softmax-style log
Blahut-Arimoto channel-capacity iteration is a normalization + reduction
polynomial interpolation evaluates Lagrange basis via FMA
spline interpolation is piecewise polynomial FMA
B-spline / NURBS evaluation is the basis for spline interpolation
Padé approximation solves a linear system for rational coefficients
model checking explores reachable states with SAT-style solvers
BDD-based model checking propagates Boolean constraints
proof search rewrites terms via resolution / superposition
unification is the matching kernel of resolution
fixed-point computation iterates constraint propagation until convergence
Dirac operator on a lattice is a sparse matrix-vector product on spinors
Dixmier trace is a singular reduction (regularized sum) over operator spectrum
cyclic cohomology is computed by reducing the cyclic complex
p-adic arithmetic is integer arithmetic with carries propagating left
Hensel lifting iterates p-adic Newton at increasing precision
subobject classifier evaluation is functor application onto Ω
Serre's intersection multiplicity formula uses Tor (computed via Gröbner)
Chow ring product is intersection product mod rational equivalence
Hybrid Monte Carlo runs Metropolis (MCMC) on top of MD trajectories
the molecular-dynamics phase of HMC is leapfrog ODE integration
the accept/reject phase is Monte Carlo
Wilson loop is an ordered product of SU(3) link variables (matmul chain)
the trace at the end of the loop is a reduction
lattice Dirac operator is sparse and complex
the standard solver is conjugate gradient — a Krylov subspace method
tropicalization rewrites polynomials in (min,+) semiring algebra
monodromy = parallel transport composed around a closed loop
residue extraction integrates around a pole
analytic continuation is symbolic Padé / series manipulation
differentiation provides Taylor coefficients for continuation
Steenrod squares are natural operations on cohomology
Moyal star product is a deformed matrix multiplication
RG block-spin step coarse-grains a lattice via PDE-style averaging
integrating out short-distance modes is a reduction
perturbative expansion is symbolic series generation
Feynman diagrams are the term-generating mechanism in QFT perturbation
Reed-Solomon decoding via Berlekamp-Massey extends generic syndrome decode
RS arithmetic is GF(2^m)
BCH decoding is syndrome decode in a polynomial ring
BCH operates over GF(2^m)
Reed-Muller decoding uses GF(2) majority logic
BCJR turbo decoding is sum-product on a trellis factor graph
fountain code decoding is belief propagation on a rateless code graph
discrete Gaussian sampling draws from RNG via rejection
the lattice basis comes from prior LLL/BKZ reduction
Vélu's isogeny formula operates on elliptic curve points
Poseidon/MiMC sponge S-boxes are GF(p) operations
the absorb / squeeze structure is hash-shaped
Shamir Lagrange interpolation is FMA in GF(p)
polynomial inversion uses modular exponentiation
threshold signature partials evaluate at curve points
VDF iterates modular exponentiation in a class group
bootstrap resampling draws indices from RNG
the resample-with-replacement loop is Monte Carlo
permutation tests draw shuffled labels from RNG
robust M-estimators take gradients of Huber/Tukey losses
IRLS is alternating proximal updates
quantile regression is solved via the LP simplex method
RANSAC randomly samples minimal inlier subsets
the consensus phase is Monte Carlo accept/reject
Gomory cutting planes augment the LP simplex
column generation iterates an LP master with a pricing subproblem
simulated annealing is Metropolis (MCMC) with a temperature schedule
the proposal step draws from RNG
tabu search compares moves against a memory of recently-tried ones
the tabu list is a constraint propagated forward
Lagrangian relaxation alternates dual ascent with proximal updates
BFGS / L-BFGS approximates the Hessian via rank-2 matrix updates
Hungarian assignment is augmenting-path search on a bipartite join
AKS primality runs a polynomial-time modular exponentiation
the polynomial congruence check is symbolic
BSGS uses a hash table for the baby-step lookups
both phases are modular exponentiations
Pollard rho iterates a pseudo-random function modulo n
index calculus solves a linear system over Z/p
the relation collection uses modular exponentiation
Lucas-Lehmer iterates squaring mod a Mersenne prime (FFT-based mul)
Burnside enumeration averages |Fix(g)| over the group — a Möbius-like sum
the orbit count is a reduction
Pólya enumeration evaluates a cycle index polynomial via Möbius/zeta
Ryser's permanent algorithm is a structured matmul-and-add
the inclusion-exclusion sum is a reduction
RSK insertion / bumping uses sort-style comparison ladders
discontinuous Galerkin extends FEM with element-local basis
boundary element method integrates a kernel over a surface mesh
the BEM linear system is dense (or hierarchical)
lattice Boltzmann's stream-and-collide step is FMA-heavy
the macroscopic moments are reductions
LB approximates a continuum PDE in the kinetic limit
SPH kernel-weighted accumulation is FMA-based
particle interactions reduce neighbor contributions
immersed boundary couples Lagrangian solid forces into an Eulerian PDE
the fluid step uses an FEM-style assembly
wavelet packet decomposition is a tree of wavelet transforms
chirplet transform extends FFT with linear-frequency-modulated atoms
Slepian / prolate spheroidal basis is computed via eigendecomposition
Gale-Shapley stable matching is a sequence of joins between proposing sets
auction algorithm runs distributed bid joins
Hopcroft-Karp augments matchings via BFS+DFS over a bipartite join
Stoer-Wagner min cut runs in the tropical semiring
float arithmetic is FMA with rounding — same datapath without the fuse
quantize/dequantize converts between float and integer representations
stochastic rounding is float rounding with an entropy injection
the stochastic bit comes from RNG
interval arithmetic pairs two directed-rounding float operations
multipole expansion uses FFT for the far-field translation
the near-field is a local reduction
embedding lookup feeds the prior/likelihood tables in Bayesian update
binary HD bind/bundle/permute extends FHRR with majority vote
LIF membrane integration is a leaky quadrature of input current
the spike threshold is a comparison
STDP reads pre/post spike times from LIF neurons
analog crossbar MAC is a physical matmul (Ohm's law)
photonic MZI implements unitary matrix transforms optically
sigma-delta feeds an analog crossbar with 1-bit oversampled signals
Gillespie draws two random samples per step from RNG
reversible arithmetic (Toffoli/Fredkin) is built from reversible Boolean gates
strand displacement is a molecular rewriting system
symplectic integrator is an area-preserving ODE step
fuzzy semiring (max,min) has the same algebraic structure as tropical
entropy coding works hand-in-hand with syndrome decoding in channel coding

bennett_decomposition.l0_phase: Hilbert transform = FFT + phase shift + iFFT, all unitary (L₀)
predict step is a linear (reversible) update (L₀)
bennett_decomposition.l2_phase: innovation covariance is reduced via Kalman gain (L₂)

bennett_decomposition.l0_phase: comparison network generating the convex-hull permutation (L₀)
shooting a geodesic from a tangent vector is locally bijective (L₀)
language reversal is a self-inverse permutation (L₀)
CG matrix-vector products are linear (mostly L₀)
bennett_decomposition.l2_phase: residual norm reduction at each step (L₂)

bennett_decomposition.l0_phase: RSK insertion / bumping is a bijection between permutations and SYT pairs (L₀)
bennett_decomposition.l2_phase: no L₂ phase — RSK is purely bijective

bennett_decomposition.l0_phase: wavelet packet QMF tree is unitary (L₀)

thermodynamic_inheritance.note: matmul inherits C2 from reduction
attention inherits C2 from softmax
attention inherits C2-max from accumulation
pooling inherits C2-max from reduction
sum-product inherits C2 from logsumexp
MCMC inherits C2 from accept/reject
PCA inherits L₂max from the covariance reduction
partition function inherits L₂max from the state-sum reduction
Bayesian posterior inherits L₂ from softmax normalization
KL divergence inherits L₂max from the log-ratio reduction
channel capacity inherits L₂max from Blahut-Arimoto reduction
Kalman filter inherits L₂ from matrix decomposition
interior point inherits L₂ from KKT factorization
LQR Riccati inherits L₂ from matrix decomposition
particle filter inherits L₂ from accept/reject
HMC inherits L₂ from Metropolis acceptance
Wilson loop inherits L₂max from the trace reduction
fermion matrix inversion inherits L₂ from Krylov residuals
permanent inherits L₂max from the inclusion-exclusion reduction
lattice Boltzmann moments inherit L₂max from neighbor reduction
